stories.headline: Explosion at China fireworks factory kills 26 people
stories.summary: A blast at a fireworks factory in Hunan province has killed 26 people and injured 61, state media report.

stories.headline: Nine killed in central Colombia mine explosion caused by gas buildup

stories.headline: Hong Kong Weathers War Shocks With Fastest Growth Since 2021
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s economy expanded at its fastest pace in almost five years despite a global energy crisis unleashed by the war in Iran, thanks to an insatiable global demand for artificial intelligence along with an uptick in both consumption and investment.

stories.headline: Erste Plans SRT Tied to Austrian Commercial Real Estate Loans
stories.summary: Erste Group Bank AG is working on a significant risk transfer tied to commercial real estate loans in Austria as regulators push lenders to bolster their capital buffers against potential defaults in the sector.

stories.headline: Ferrari Profit Beats Views as Higher Prices Outweigh Volume Drop
stories.summary: Ferrari NV reported a rise in first-quarter earnings that beat analysts’ estimates, as personal add-ons and a richer mix of supercars outweighed a wartime drop in deliveries.

stories.headline: JPMorgan’s Matejka Sees More Stock Gains After Narrow Rally
stories.summary: The rebound in global stocks from their Iran war lows has been so narrow that the market is primed for broader gains triggered by even slightly positive news, according to strategists at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

stories.headline: France Says EU Will Retaliate If US Threatens Its Industries
stories.summary: The European Union has tools that it can turn to if Donald Trump makes excessive threats to strategic industries such as steel, French Trade Minister Nicolas Forissier said.

stories.headline: UK to Introduce 12-Month Treasury Bills to Broaden Debt Market
stories.summary: The UK will begin selling 12-month Treasury bills at its weekly auctions, as part of the government’s efforts to tilt its borrowing toward shorter-maturity debt.

stories.headline: What to Know About the California Primary Election
stories.summary: Voters are starting to make decisions after receiving their primary ballots this week. The election includes a turbulent governor’s race and a competitive mayoral contest in Los Angeles.

stories.headline: Pakistan’s Trade Deficit Widens to Highest in Four Years

stories.headline: Alphabet Returns to Euro Debt Market for Latest AI Megabond Deal

stories.headline: Vodafone to Buy Out UK’s Biggest Operator for $5.8 Billion
stories.summary: Vodafone Group Plc is buying CK Hutchison Holdings Ltd. stake in the UK’s largest mobile operator in a deal worth £4.3 billion ($5.8 billion), as Chief Executive Officer Margherita Della Valle accelerates a plan to consolidate in the company’s biggest markets.

stories.headline: AMD Earnings Need to Justify Rally After Best Month in 25 Years
stories.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is coming off its best month in the stock market since the dot-com era, and with the chipmaker’s first-quarter earnings due after the bell, investors will get a chance to see whether its shares have gotten ahead of its fundamentals.

stories.headline: AI’s Hottest Private Firms Have Booming Crypto Shadow Market
stories.summary: The race to sell retail investors a piece of the AI boom has gone mainstream — closed-end funds, interval funds, special-purpose vehicles. Now, crypto platforms are offering trades tied to the most valuable private AI companies on earth — ones ordinary investors have almost no other way to access.

stories.headline: Japan Has Two More Windows for Yen Intervention by IMF Rules
stories.summary: Japan can conduct only two more sessions of three-day interventions by November if it wants to maintain its status of having a freely floating exchange rate, based on International Monetary Fund guidelines.

stories.headline: Can Ken Paxton Win a Senate Primary in Texas Without His Biggest Donors?
